world_date: Month of the Iron Threshold, Year 1 of the Hollow Accord
major_event: The sealed mine-gate in Lostfeld opens on its own. No mechanism was triggered. No dwarf stood near it. It opened inward, into darkness, and a sound emerged — not the vibration, but a voice. One word, in a language older than dwarven script. Kessa Stonebraid was the only one close enough to hear it. She has not repeated it to anyone.

recent_events.text: The sealed mine-gate swung open at the third hour past midnight. No mechanism fired. Ironmaul ordered it shut and barred with iron — the bar bent outward within the hour. The gate now stands open.

Lythara convened Clan Arkenor's war-council without Velorn or Nythariel present. The agenda was not recorded. By dawn, Arkenor scouts were moving east — toward the Frostvale ridge paths Elowen's war-bands now occupy.
Caeris Thorne received Aldric's courier. He read the letter once, burned it, and requested an audience with the Frostvale war-council — the first formal meeting he has sought since arriving.

Roderic signed the ultimatum. The thirty-day clock to Tidefall has begun. Within hours, Tidefall's harbor envoy delivered a counter-letter: the ships will not move, but the Admiral invites negotiation aboard his flagship.

Rowen's depth-mapping ships returned. The charts they carry show a submerged structure beneath the Tidefall outer lanes — geometric, deliberate, and very old. Rowen has gone quiet. He has not shared the charts.

active_tensions.factions: Lostfeld Dwarves — Deep Entity vs All Surface Factions
active_tensions.description: The gate is open. The entity has spoken. Ironmaul's containment has failed. Whatever is below Lostfeld is no longer waiting — and the symbols on Kessa link it to forces already active above ground.

active_tensions.factions: Shadow Court vs Glenhaven Elves + Caeris Thorne
active_tensions.description: Arkenor scouts moving toward Frostvale ridge paths risk direct contact with Elowen's redeployed war-bands. A skirmish would collapse the covert alliance before it is ever formalized.

active_tensions.factions: Twin Cities — Roderic vs Tidefall vs Aldric + Dur Khadur
active_tensions.description: The signed ultimatum forces Tidefall's hand. Aldric's Dur Khadur link and Caeris's Frostvale contact mean three separate Thorne factions now run independent foreign policies simultaneously.

active_tensions.factions: Dreadwind Islands — Rowen vs Tidefall (Submerged Structure)
active_tensions.description: Rowen's charts reveal something beneath Tidefall's lanes. If the structure is a weapon or anchor-point, whoever controls it controls the sea routes. Rowen has not yet decided who to sell this knowledge to.

character_updates.name: Kessa Stonebraid
character_updates.status: Heard the voice from the open gate. Has not repeated the word spoken. The symbols on her neck have stopped spreading — they now pulse in sync with something below. She is functional but unreachable.

character_updates.name: Thane Ulric Ironmaul
character_updates.status: The bent bar has broken him visibly. He has sent an emergency sealed missive to three factions — Gloomspire Gnomes, Monastery of Druids, and one unknown recipient. He looks like a man writing wills.

character_updates.name: Sovereign Elowen Silverleaf
character_updates.status: War-bands on the ridge have reported Arkenor scouts eastward. Elowen has not ordered engagement. She has sent a single rider back toward Frostvale — carrying nothing written. Only a spoken message.

character_updates.name: Prince Caeris Thorne
character_updates.status: Burned Aldric's letter. Requested formal audience with Frostvale council. Has begun asking about the mountain pass routes — specifically which ones the Gnomes do not control.

character_updates.name: King Roderic Thorne II
character_updates.status: Signed the ultimatum. The act cost him — his hand shook. He has retreated to his private library and cancelled all audiences for three days. The court is governing itself.

character_updates.name: Pirate King Rowen
character_updates.status: Sits alone with the depth charts. Has eaten nothing since the ships returned. Three of his senior captains have asked to see the charts and been refused. He is deciding something enormous.

faction_morale.reason: The gate is open. Three foremen who requested transfer have now simply vanished from their posts. The word 'desertion' has not been used yet. It will be.
Arkenor scouts move with purpose for the first time in months. Lythara's court reads this as aggression restored. Only she knows it may already be too late to control Arkenor's momentum.
War-bands on the ridge hold discipline. Elowen's silence in the face of Arkenor scouts reads as confidence to her soldiers, though her inner council is less certain.
The signed ultimatum has not unified the court — it has fractured it. Aldric's faction sees it as overreach. Roderic's withdrawal worsens the vacuum.
The ultimatum was expected. The Admiral's counter-invitation to his flagship is a calculated move — negotiation on his terms, in his water, aboard his ship.
Ironmaul's sealed missive arrived in Stonebreak. The Grand Druid read it and immediately ended his fast — not from hope, but from urgency. The Conclave is being recalled.
Rowen's silence after the charts' return has unsettled the fleet. Victory was expected. Instead their king sits alone and refuses counsel. Faith wavers when kings go quiet.
Ironmaul's missive to the Syndicate has arrived. Its contents are unknown to outsiders, but the Syndicate has quietly activated two pass-crossing teams. Something is being moved — or someone.

whispers.text: The word the entity spoke from the open gate is not unknown — it is the name carved above the Monastery's oldest sealed door. The Grand Druid has known it for forty years.
The submerged structure beneath the Tidefall lanes has a twin. Rowen's instruments recorded a second shape — smaller, closer to the sinking island. He has told no one.
Aldric's letter to Caeris was not a threat. It was an offer: Dur Khadur backing for Caeris's claim to the Twin Cities throne, in exchange for one undisclosed future concession.
Ironmaul's third sealed missive — not to the Gnomes, not to the Druids — was addressed to a name no one in Lostfeld has spoken aloud in a generation. The courier who carried it has not returned.

weather_and_omens.condition: Since the gate opened, the vibration has ceased entirely. The sudden silence is worse. Veteran miners say the mountain is listening now, not speaking.
The drifting moon-shadow has stopped moving. It rests over the eastern ridge — precisely where Arkenor scouts and Elowen's war-bands are converging.
The upward-falling snow has returned, but now it forms shapes before dissolving — letters, perhaps, in an alphabet no one has identified. They last only seconds.
The Sealed Garden novice pressed both hands against the warm wall at dawn and the stone cracked along a perfect line — revealing a hollow behind it. It was empty, but recently so.
The Eldoria harbor water has turned faintly luminescent at night — only near the hulls of the three Tidefall warships. Fishermen are refusing to work after dark.

absorbed_lore: The sealed mine-gate in Lostfeld opened on its own — the iron bar bent outward and the gate now stands permanently open.
The entity below spoke one word in an ancient language; only Kessa Stonebraid heard it and she has not repeated it.
The symbols on Kessa's neck have stopped spreading but now pulse in rhythm with something beneath the mountain.
Ironmaul sent sealed missives to the Gloomspire Gnomes, the Monastery of Druids, and one unknown third recipient — the courier for the third has not returned.
Lythara convened an Arkenor-only war-council; by dawn scouts were moving east toward the Frostvale ridge paths.
Elowen has not ordered engagement with Arkenor scouts — she sent a single rider to Frostvale carrying only a spoken message, nothing written.
Caeris burned Aldric's letter and requested formal Frostvale council audience; he is asking about mountain passes the Gnomes do not control.
Aldric's letter to Caeris was an offer: Dur Khadur backing for his throne claim in exchange for one undisclosed future concession.
Roderic signed the ultimatum; his hand shook; he has since cancelled all audiences and retreated to his library.
Tidefall's counter to the ultimatum: the ships will not move, but the Admiral invites Roderic to negotiate aboard his flagship.
Rowen's depth-mapping ships returned with charts showing a submerged geometric structure — deliberate and ancient — beneath the Tidefall outer lanes.
Rowen has shared the charts with no one; his instruments also recorded a second smaller structure near the sinking island, which he has told no one about.
The Gloomspire Syndicate has activated two pass-crossing teams in response to Ironmaul's missive.
The word spoken by the entity matches the name carved above the Monastery's oldest sealed door — the Grand Druid has known it for forty years.
The Sealed Garden wall cracked to reveal a hollow behind it — empty, but recently occupied by something.
